column1: # By default, v2ecoli routes a per-gene fold-change row from
# fold_changes_nca.tsv to the gene's canonical surviving TU (the one
# that survived the gene_tuple-keyed dedup in getter_functions.py). For
# operons where biology dictates a specific TF binds only a subset of
# the operon's promoters, this file overrides that default routing.
# Used by Phase 3 of the Path 3 implementation in
#   TF:          TF short name (matches the TF column in
#   target_gene: gene short name the fold-change is annotated against
#   TU_id:       the TU id to route the fold-change to (replaces the
#   route_only:  if "true", apply the fold-change ONLY to the listed
#                TU (not to the canonical surviving TU). If "false",
#                apply additively (both this TU AND the canonical).
#   source:      where the routing decision came from (paper or "manual")
#   (lexA, dnaG) → TU00435 — Janion 2008 and primary characterization
#   of rpsUp3 as the LexA-responsive SOS promoter; ratio file confirms
#   rpsUp3 is silent under non-SOS conditions, so the per-gene
